methodology.mapping: Scores are mapped into JobOrBot's replaceability scale using conservative percentile bands from Microsoft's occupation-score distribution rather than linear max-normalization.
methodology.note: Because the Microsoft source measures AI usefulness within occupational tasks, not end-to-end job replacement, this variant intentionally tops out at 8/10 rather than claiming any occupation is fully replaceable today.

target_scale.name: JobOrBot replaceability scale
target_scale.question: How close is AI to being able to do the whole occupation today, rather than only assist with parts of it?

anchors.meaning: AI is barely useful for doing the real job today.
AI is materially useful across substantial parts of the job, but still far from replacing it.
AI can effectively do the whole job today with minimal human help.
